questions.question_text: Which question is the most focused investigation about a puri puffing in hot oil?

options.text: Is science useful?
How does one chosen condition affect time taken to puff up?
What is the most beautiful thing in nature?
Will every object in the world behave the same way?

questions.explanation: A focused question identifies a condition and an observable outcome.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: In an investigation of a puri puffing in hot oil, which condition is the independent variable if the student changes it deliberately?

options.text: the puri puffed in 18 seconds
time taken to puff up
oil temperature
The colour of the notebook

questions.explanation: The independent variable is the condition deliberately changed by the investigator.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: What should be measured when investigating a puri puffing in hot oil?

options.text: The name of the classroom
oil temperature
The student's favourite result
time taken to puff up

questions.explanation: A dependent variable is an observable or measurable outcome.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: Which condition should be kept the same for a fair test of a puri puffing in hot oil?

options.text: same dough thickness and drop method
oil temperature
time taken to puff up
A new condition in every trial

questions.explanation: Controlled variables are kept constant so the effect of the chosen change can be compared fairly.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: Which statement is an observation in an investigation of a puri puffing in hot oil?

options.text: steam formed inside the dough may push its layers apart
the puri puffed in 18 seconds
The result must always be the same
The investigator feels curious

questions.explanation: An observation records what was noticed or measured, without adding an unsupported cause.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: Which is a testable explanation for the observation in an investigation of a puri puffing in hot oil?

options.text: Science can answer every question immediately
The result is beautiful
steam formed inside the dough may push its layers apart
The investigator should ignore all results

questions.explanation: A testable explanation connects the observation to a possible cause that can be investigated.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: Why should a student change only one main condition at a time while investigating a puri puffing in hot oil?

options.text: So every result is guaranteed before testing
So no measurements are needed
So the investigation becomes an opinion
So the effect of that condition can be compared more clearly

questions.explanation: Changing one main condition helps link a difference in the result to that condition.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: A student changes the oil temperature and also changes the dough thickness and drop method at once. What is the main problem?

options.text: The student cannot tell which change caused the result
The student has too many observations
The experiment automatically becomes safer
The result becomes a scientific law

questions.explanation: Changing multiple conditions makes it difficult to identify the cause of a difference.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: Which record would be most useful after observing a puri puffing in hot oil?

options.text: A drawing with no labels or date
A dated table containing the changed condition and time taken to puff up
A memory written several weeks later
Only the result the student expected

questions.explanation: Organised, dated records allow results to be compared and checked.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: A first trial in an investigation of a puri puffing in hot oil gives an unexpected result. What is the best next step?

options.text: Change every condition without recording it
Delete the result because it is unexpected
Check the notes and repeat the trial while keeping the planned conditions consistent
Declare the explanation proven immediately

questions.explanation: Unexpected results should be checked, recorded, and investigated rather than hidden or treated as proof.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: Which condition should be kept the same for a fair test of a breeze moving a paper strip?

options.text: angle through which the strip moves
the distance from a fan
same paper strip and fan setting
A new condition in every trial

questions.explanation: Controlled variables are kept constant so the effect of the chosen change can be compared fairly.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: Which statement is an observation in an investigation of a breeze moving a paper strip?

options.text: The investigator feels curious
air movement may be weaker farther from the fan
The result must always be the same
the strip moved through 35 degrees

questions.explanation: An observation records what was noticed or measured, without adding an unsupported cause.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: Which is a testable explanation for the observation in an investigation of a breeze moving a paper strip?

options.text: air movement may be weaker farther from the fan
The result is beautiful
Science can answer every question immediately
The investigator should ignore all results

questions.explanation: A testable explanation connects the observation to a possible cause that can be investigated.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: Why should a student change only one main condition at a time while investigating a breeze moving a paper strip?

options.text: So no measurements are needed
So the effect of that condition can be compared more clearly
So the investigation becomes an opinion
So every result is guaranteed before testing

questions.explanation: Changing one main condition helps link a difference in the result to that condition.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: A student changes the distance from a fan and also changes the paper strip and fan setting at once. What is the main problem?

options.text: The experiment automatically becomes safer
The student has too many observations
The student cannot tell which change caused the result
The result becomes a scientific law

questions.explanation: Changing multiple conditions makes it difficult to identify the cause of a difference.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: Which record would be most useful after observing a breeze moving a paper strip?

options.text: Only the result the student expected
A drawing with no labels or date
A memory written several weeks later
A dated table containing the changed condition and angle through which the strip moves

questions.explanation: Organised, dated records allow results to be compared and checked.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: A first trial in an investigation of a breeze moving a paper strip gives an unexpected result. What is the best next step?

options.text: Check the notes and repeat the trial while keeping the planned conditions consistent
Delete the result because it is unexpected
Change every condition without recording it
Declare the explanation proven immediately

questions.explanation: Unexpected results should be checked, recorded, and investigated rather than hidden or treated as proof.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: Which question is the most focused investigation about a shadow changing during the day?

options.text: Is science useful?
How does one chosen condition affect length of the shadow?
What is the most beautiful thing in nature?
Will every object in the world behave the same way?

questions.explanation: A focused question identifies a condition and an observable outcome.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: In an investigation of a shadow changing during the day, which condition is the independent variable if the student changes it deliberately?

options.text: the shadow measured 48 centimetres at noon
length of the shadow
time of day
The colour of the notebook

questions.explanation: The independent variable is the condition deliberately changed by the investigator.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

questions.question_text: What should be measured when investigating a shadow changing during the day?

options.text: The name of the classroom
time of day
The student's favourite result
length of the shadow

questions.explanation: A dependent variable is an observable or measurable outcome.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 1, pages 1–7; original question derived from the investigation skills

The colour of the notebook
